content.thinking:
Three consistent bench runs:
- Run 1: 208.9 tok/s
- Run 2: 214.4 tok/s
- Run 3: 215.0 tok/s

Average: ~212.8 tok/s (up from 132.8 baseline). That's a 60% improvement!

Parity with MLX reference: ~90%.

This is a clear, consistent win. Let me keep this experiment.

content.text: Consistently strong results across 3 runs: 208.9, 214.4, 215.0 tok/s (avg ~213 tok/s, up from 132.8 baseline — **+60%**). Parity with MLX reference at 90%. Let me keep this.

input.summary: Interleaved Q4 weight reads across SIMD lanes for fully coalesced memory transactions. Changed all 9 Q4 kernels from strided access (lane l reads contiguous block at offset l*cols_per_lane, 32-byte inter-lane stride, 12.5% cache line utilization) to interleaved access (lane l reads word at w*32+lane, 4-byte inter-lane stride, 100% cache line utilization). Added per-word scale/bias accumulation since interleaved words span multiple groups. Changed fused norm kernels from padded TG memory layout to linear layout. Result: decode 132.8 → 213 tok/s average (+60%), parity with MLX reference 90%. Prefill also improved 78 → 97 tok/s.
